notes: Phantom displays the fee on the confirmation screen of every send; user confirms before sending.
Phantom supports transfer-hook tokens; it builds the extra hook accounts so sends succeed.
Phantom displays a warning for any token with this extension (the delegate can burn or transfer any amount).
Phantom displays the current interest rate on the token's detail screen.
Phantom supports required-memo-on-transfer and lets users input a memo on transfers.
Phantom follows the metadata pointer to render name/symbol/image.
Phantom reads name and symbol from the mint's metadata extension and fetches the image from the uri field.
Phantom uses group/group-pointer info to display members under the Collectibles tab.
Group extension recognized for associating tokens with a parent collection.
Member/group-pointer relationship used to associate a token with its group for display.
Member metadata used to group collectibles, matched against the group address.
Recognized extension; balances render normally, no special send restriction documented.
Phantom acknowledges confidential transfer, but encrypted balances do not render like normal balances; display is partial.
Sub-extension of confidential transfer; same partial-display caveat for encrypted amounts.
Token displays, but if the account is frozen-by-default the send fails until thawed (mechanical inference).
Display-only UI scaling; Phantom renders Token-2022 mints, so the scaled amount is expected to display (low confidence, not separately documented).
Account-level extension; does not block display or normal sends.
Token displays, but sends fail while the mint is paused (mechanical inference).
Non-transferable tokens cannot be sent: the Token-2022 program rejects any Transfer instruction. Phantom displays the balance but the send fails.
Phantom renders any Token-2022 mint; an unrecognized extension still displays as a balance (no documented block).
Phantom has the most thoroughly documented Token-2022 support of any consumer Solana wallet, with a dedicated developer page enumerating per-extension behavior.

Wallet context: 'supported' = displays and sends fine, 'conditional' = shows a warning or depends on state, 'blocked' = cannot send. Extensions not individually documented by Phantom are mechanical inferences at low confidence.
